calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-24-2022 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2022 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jul-19-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-17-2022 Continued To Jun-08-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JAMIE TOWNSEND of the Law Offices of Jamie Townsend (Tel: 760-612-9707) for plaintiff NPU Inc.;
Atty. DANIEL M. PONIATOWKSI, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-421-6500) for defendants Exhibition Hub, Exhibition Hub, Inc., Exhibition Hub Management LLC, Exhibition Hub US Holdings, LLC, and Exhibition Hub Los Angeles, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS PHH MORTGAGE CORPORATION, HSBC BANK USA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, AND WESTERN PROGRESSIVE TRUSTEE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling with modifications, as follows:

Defendants' demurrers to the complaint's numbered causes of action are sustained with leave to amend.

1 & 3: The complaint fails to adequately identify the contractual provisions and/or material terms under which defendants were obligated. (Performance Plastering v. Richmond American Homes of Cal., Inc. (2007) 153 Cal. App. 4th 659, 671.)

4: The complaint fails to adequately plead facts that do not merely duplicate the breach of contract cause of action. (Careau & Co. v. Security Pacific Business Credit (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1371, 1392-1400.)

6: The complaint does not plead specific facts that defendants participated in an independently wrongful act designed to disrupt the business relationship.

7: Misrepresentation is not pled with adequate specificity. (Service by Medallion, Inc. v. Clorox Co. (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1807, 1816.)

8: The class definitions are so broad as to be meaningless.

The demurrer as to the fifth cause of action is overruled.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-08-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. JAMIE TOWNSEND of the Law Offices of Jamie Townsend (Tel: 760-612-9707) for plaintiff NPU Inc.;
Atty. DANIEL M. PONIATOWKSI, Esq. of Shartsis Friese LLP (Tel: 415-421-6500) for defendants Exhibition Hub, Exhibition Hub, Inc., Exhibition Hub Management LLC, Exhibition Hub US Holdings, LLC, and Exhibition Hub Los Angeles, LLC.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS EXHIBITION HUB, EXHIBITION HUB INC., EXHIBITION HUB MANAGEMENT LLC, EXHIBITION HUB US HOLDING LLC, AND EXHIBITION HUB LOS ANGELES LLC'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' motion to strike is granted. Plaintiff asserts this cause of action behalf of the general public based on the theory Defendants actions led to the reduction in competition and consumer choice in Los Angeles County. (Complaint 52.) Under this theory, the Plaintiff does not and cannot meet the class action requirements of CCP sec. 382, as well as the requirements for a representative action under Bus. & Prof. Code sec. 17200 et seq. (See Arias v. Superior Court (2009) 46 Cal. 4th 969, 980 (representative UCL plaintiff must comply with requirements for class action representatives).

Order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/RBU)
